skill.name: grant-funding-scout
skill.description: NIH funding trend analysis to identify high-priority research areas.
skill.summary: Analyze NIH funding patterns to identify high-priority research areas and strategic grant opportunities.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 49 static findings across 4 files (692 lines). All 15 HIGH-severity 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are FALSE POSITIVES - no cryptographic code exists in the skill; the scanner misidentified argparse usage and JSON metadata text fields. The 29 MEDIUM 'Ruby/shell backtick' findings are markdown code blocks showing harmless bash commands (python compile, help) - the external_commands risk is valid but low. Remaining LOW findings (system reconnaissance, path traversal, hardcoded URL) are all documentation text or security checklist items. Final risk level is LOW - this is a legitimate research analysis tool using mock data with no malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: External Commands Documented for AI Execution
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md contains multiple bash commands (python compile, help) intended for AI agent execution. The commands are safe and use hardcoded arguments, but the pattern of external command execution by AI warrants awareness.

medium_findings.title: Python File Write Operation
medium_findings.description: Script writes output JSON to a user-specified file path via argparse --output argument. Standard output pattern for a data analysis tool, but uses a user-controlled path without validation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm in JSON Metadata
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' in grant-funding-scout_audit_result_v2.json at lines 72 and 77. These lines contain legacy audit note text about security and maintainability deductions, not any cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm in argparse
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at scripts/main.py:126. This line contains argparse.ArgumentParser instantiation, which is a standard CLI argument parser, not a cryptographic function.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm in SKILL.md Frontmatter
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md lines 3, 26, and 92. These are YAML frontmatter description, feature list text, and a table header row respectively. No cryptographic code is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'System reconnaissance' at multiple locations in audit_result_v2.json and SKILL.md. These are evaluation metadata fields and documentation text describing parameters and error handling, not actual reconnaissance commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Path Traversal in Security Checklist
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Path traversal sequence' at SKILL.md lines 132 and 135. These lines are part of a security checklist documenting that path traversal should be prevented. They describe security controls, not actual vulnerable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL in Source Citation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Hardcoded URL' at SKILL.md:7. This is a citation link pointing to the source GitHub repository. It is a documentation reference, not a network request or data exfiltration endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unvalidated Output File Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The script writes output to a user-controlled file path (args.output) without validating for directory traversal or ensuring the path stays within the workspace. While low risk in typical usage, it could write outside the intended output directory.

content.user_title: Find High-Priority NIH Funding Areas for Your Research
content.value_statement: Identifying high-priority research areas for grant funding is time-consuming and requires tracking multiple funding trends. This skill analyzes NIH funding patterns to help researchers focus their grant strategy on growing opportunities and avoid oversaturated fields.
actual_capabilities: Analyze funding trends across research areas including AI/ML, CRISPR, and Immunotherapy
Identify rising, stable, and declining funding categories with percentage changes
Suggest relevant NIH institutes based on your specific research area
Generate structured funding scout reports with priority level indicators
Detect cross-disciplinary funding opportunities and related research areas
limitations: Uses mock funding data for demonstration - not connected to live NIH databases
Only analyzes predefined research categories included in the package
Does not include specific grant dollar amounts or application deadlines
Coverage is limited to NIH institutes and does not include NSF or other agencies

use_cases.title: Strategic Research Focus
use_cases.description: A research scientist uses funding trend data to decide which research direction to pursue for maximum funding potential.

use_cases.title: Grant Application Positioning
use_cases.description: A grant writer analyzes which NIH institutes are most likely to fund a specific research area and tailors applications accordingly.

use_cases.title: Department Research Planning
use_cases.description: A department head reviews broad funding trends to allocate resources toward high-growth research areas.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Funding Trend Inquiry
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze funding trends for cancer immunotherapy research over the past 3 years. Identify the top funding areas and their growth rates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a single research area analysis

prompt_templates.title: Research Area Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare funding trends between CRISPR and AI/ML research areas. Show which has higher priority and funding growth.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Institute Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: I am planning a grant application for neurological disorders research. Suggest relevant NIH institutes and analyze the funding trajectory for brain-related research.
prompt_templates.scenario: Identifying appropriate funding bodies for a grant application

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Disciplinary Opportunity Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze funding trends for AI applications in cancer research. Identify cross-disciplinary funding opportunities and suggest which NIH institute would be the best fit.
prompt_templates.scenario: Exploring interdisciplinary research with broader funding potential

output_examples.input: What are the current funding trends in immunotherapy?
output_examples.output: Funding Scout Report shows immunotherapy as a rising trend with 35% funding increase and high priority designation. Suggested institutes include NCI (National Cancer Institute) with excellent fit.

output_examples.input: Compare funding for CRISPR and Microbiome research.
output_examples.output: CRISPR shows stable trend with 15% funding change (high priority). Microbiome shows rising trend with 28% funding change (medium priority). Both identified as active research areas with different growth trajectories.

best_practices: Specify a concrete research area for the most relevant funding analysis results
Use the broad trends overview first to understand the funding landscape before deep analysis
Save output reports to a file when sharing results with collaborators or grant teams
anti_patterns: Asking for real-time funding data - this version uses mock data for educational purposes only
Expecting specific grant dollar amounts or numbered funding opportunities - not included in this analysis
Using vague research topics instead of specific area names for best results

faq.question: Does this skill connect to live NIH funding databases?
faq.answer: No. This version uses mock funding data for educational and demonstration purposes only.

faq.question: What research areas can I analyze?
faq.answer: The skill covers AI/ML, CRISPR, Immunotherapy, Microbiome, Single Cell, and Structural Biology.

faq.question: Can this skill help me write a grant application?
faq.answer: It provides funding trend analysis to inform your strategy but does not write grant applications.

faq.question: Which NIH institutes are covered?
faq.answer: NCI, NIGMS, NHLBI, NIAID, NIAMS, and NINDS are included in the analysis.

faq.question: How current is the funding data?
faq.answer: The data is static mock data and is not updated from live funding sources.

faq.question: Can I add custom research areas to the analysis?
faq.answer: No. The skill only analyzes the predefined research categories included in the package.
